input.evidence_text: A "remarkable" 123-year-old youth club building made famous after featuring on a Smiths album sleeve has had its listed status upgraded from Grade II to Grade II* Salford Lads Club was built in the city on Coronation Street in 1903 and has helped generations of boys - and later girls - by offering activities like sport, art and music It comes less than two years after the club was saved from closure after a fundraising campaign raised £250,000, including donations from local music legends like Morrissey and Graham Nash Meanwhile, Crossley House in Openshaw, Manchester, formerly the Crossley Lads' Club, has been added to the National Heritage List for England at Grade II by the government Historic England said the Department for Culture, Media and Sport's decision, following its advice, recognised the two rare surviving examples of purpose-built lads' clubs, buildings once common in industrial towns and cities but now increasingly scarce Historic England's Emma Squire and Claudia Kenyatta said the "two remarkable buildings tell the story of a movement that transformed opportunities for generations of young people"

title: Firms' adverts disguised as parking tickets banned

input.lead: The Advertising Standards Authority said two adverts caused "unjustifiable distress"
input.summary: The Advertising Standards Authority said two adverts caused "unjustifiable distress"
input.evidence_text: Two companies' promotional fliers that were designed to look like parking tickets have been banned by the advertising watchdog The Advertising Standards Authority (ASA) said it had upheld complaints made about leaflets from the indoor golf venue Golf Fang Liverpool, and Parc, a car parking app ASA ruled the adverts, which were all wrapped in yellow and black plastic and placed on windscreens in Liverpool and Manchester, risked causing "unjustifiable distress" Golf Fang subsequently said it would not re-run what it described as "a short promotional campaign". Parc apologised and said it would make sure future campaigns were "obviously identifiable" as adverts from the outset ASA said the Golf Fang leaflet was first seen in April and featured the words "PENALTY CHARGE NOTICED ENCLOSED", and "WARNING, it is an offence for any person other than the driver to remove this notice" But inside the flier was an assurance that the driver had not been fined, and instead listed a link to the company's website

title: Wigan cabbie says 'crazy' policy will 'put me out of work'

input.lead: A taxi driver has said he and other private-hire cabbies are being "forced out of business" because their cars fall foul of clean air rules, despite passing emissions tests John Moreton, 63, from Wigan, will be unable to use his hybrid vehicle beyond 2026, because of a council po
input.summary: A taxi driver has said he and other private-hire cabbies are being "forced out of business" because their cars fall foul of clean air rules, despite passing emissions tests John Moreton, 63, from Wigan, will be unable to use his hybrid vehicle beyond 2026, because of a council po
input.evidence_text: A taxi driver has said he and other private-hire cabbies are being "forced out of business" because their cars fall foul of clean air rules, despite passing emissions tests John Moreton, 63, from Wigan, will be unable to use his hybrid vehicle beyond 2026, because of a council policy banning cars older than 12-years-old from being used as private-hire taxis "This rule will put me out of work and onto benefits, it's crazy", said Moreton Wigan Council said its age policy ensured vehicles were "safe, reliable, and environmentally sustainable". However, it did say it would give "further consideration" to drivers' concerns Moreton said his "pristine" 17-year-old Toyota Prius had passed three separate MOTs in the past 12 months, two of which were requested by Wigan Council "To buy a new taxi will cost me between £20-25,000. Would I really want to take on that finance at 63 years old?" he said

input.evidence_text: A woman who repeatedly drove over a man lying in a road after failing to defrost her car windscreen during a winter's morning has been jailed for six years Megan Murphy, 26, pleaded guilty to causing the death of 31-year-old Darryl Tomlinson by dangerous driving. He had collapsed on Pembroke Road, Wigan, in the hours before he was hit by the car on 9 January 2025 Witnesses tried to alert Murphy as she drove towards Tomlinson, with one shouting "stop" and "go back" and another sounding a car horn Bolton Crown Court heard Murphy later called a friend and said: "I've ran him over but it's a smackhead." Murphy, of Lord Street, Wigan, had got into her car at about 05:47 GMT on the icy morning and reversed from an alleyway, facing the wrong way down the one-way street, Greater Manchester Police said CCTV showed her windscreen and windows were visibly frozen, significantly obstructing her view of the road

title: TV Festival to move from Edinburgh to Manchester after 50 years

input.lead: The TV Festival is relocating from Edinburgh to Manchester next year after half a century Organisers said it was moving from the Scottish capital "amid increasing challenges around accessibility, affordability and sustainability" across the television industry The TV Foundation
input.summary: The TV Festival is relocating from Edinburgh to Manchester next year after half a century Organisers said it was moving from the Scottish capital "amid increasing challenges around accessibility, affordability and sustainability" across the television industry The TV Foundation

title: Bury woman murdered her husband, 84, who had dementia

input.lead: Daryl Berman is found guilty despite claiming her husband had accidentally sustained his injuries
input.summary: Daryl Berman is found guilty despite claiming her husband had accidentally sustained his injuries
input.evidence_text: A woman murdered her 84-year-old husband who had dementia and then tried to cover up her crime by claiming the fatal stab wound was accidental Minshull Street Crown Court in Manchester heard that Daryl Berman, 72, stabbed her husband of 27 years in the chest at their home in Bury on 13 March She had claimed David Berman's injuries had been sustained as he fell carrying a tray with a knife into the kitchen, but on Tuesday the jury found her guilty of murder After the case, senior crown prosecutor Sazeeda Ismail said: "The jury saw through her lies and convicted her of murder, due to the strength of the prosecution's case." Berman had "lied to paramedics, police and family members when she claimed Mr Berman had accidentally sustained his fatal injuries", she added She will be sentenced at the same court on 3 July

title: England over-75s to face Germany for 1966 World Cup anniversary match

input.lead: The team, known as the Silver Lions, are training in York ahead of the game at the end of July
input.summary: The team, known as the Silver Lions, are training in York ahead of the game at the end of July
input.evidence_text: A group of footballers who make up England's over-75s side is gearing up for a match against Germany to mark the 60th anniversary of the country's only World Cup win The team, known as the Silver Lions, have worked alongside 1966 World Cup winner Sir Geoff Hurst as part of preparations for the game on 30 July at Boundary Park, Oldham "It's normal football, 11-a-side, on a full-size pitch, same rules as always and it's exciting and enjoyable at our age to still be involved in football," said team member Johnny Molloy The 77-year-old's niece Tracy Willits, a lecturer at the University of York St John, organised the game as part of a documentary she is making "It's always fascinated me that at that age people are playing that level of sport, so I thought it would make a nice documentary," she said "I had a word with the Germans, they were up for it and now it's built up to this massive occasion and quite frankly I am very nervous about it, but I am also very excited," she adds

'Frustrated' Eric Mwangi, 35, carried out the crime spree earlier this year. Minshull Street Crown Court heard he was due to be released from prison on Wednesday (June 24) Prosecuting, Amy Weir said Mwangi struck at a car park on Pomona Strand in Trafford . Windscreens were smashed with a metal bar. Ms Weir said almost £4,000-worth of damage was caused. The incident happened at around 11.45pm on March 2 this year Mwangi pleaded guilty to 15 counts of criminal damage. The court heard how he has 11 previous convictions covering 28 offences, including other offences of criminal damage "This appears to be a revenge attack of sorts," Ms Weir said. Defending, Samrat Sengupta described the incident as a 'frustrated rampage'. He said the defendant was experiencing mental health issues and homelessness at the time Sentencing, Judge Jason MacAdam said he intended to pass a sentence that would mean Mwangi would be released from prison with no more time to serve. Mr Sengupta did not offer any further mitigation following the indication from the judge

A design and access report in support of the care home plans has been published It said: “The decision has been taken to look at an alternative option, with the submission of an application for a residential care home. It will affect large parts of the country including Greater Manchester, the Midlands, Yorkshire and South East England The Met Office's amber alert reads: "Hot and humid weather is expected to continue, with exceptionally warm temperatures both day and night." It continued: "Very hot weather will continue to affect much of England. title: What it means for tenants as Bury council considers social housing sell-off

input.lead: Bury What it means for tenants as Bury council considers social housing sell-off A number of options are being weighed up Comments News Hannah Richardson 11:22, 24 Jun 2026 View Image More than 100 homes could be impacted (Image: MEN) Bury council is considering selling off more
input.summary: Bury What it means for tenants as Bury council considers social housing sell-off A number of options are being weighed up Comments News Hannah Richardson 11:22, 24 Jun 2026 View Image More than 100 homes could be impacted (Image: MEN) Bury council is considering selling off more
input.evidence_text: Bury What it means for tenants as Bury council considers social housing sell-off A number of options are being weighed up Comments News Hannah Richardson 11:22, 24 Jun 2026 View Image More than 100 homes could be impacted (Image: MEN) Bury council is considering selling off more than 100 social housing properties The move is among the options being considered as town hall bosses look to wind up Six Town Housing Ltd (STH). The closure of the social housing provider was first mooted in 2024, with the authority bringing most of the 7,800 managed by STH back in-house Some 117 specialist properties continue to be managed by STH. The council is now weighing up the future of those homes Five possibilities were put forward by the Bury council. Two would see the remaining stock disposed of to a new registered provider either by selling the homes or by transferring STH into their management. A further two would see the homes brought in-house and managed by the auth The fifth would have seen tenants evicted and the homes sold on the open market. However, Bury council said it has ruled this option out as it would have placed increased pressure on the council’s homelessness services as new housing is sought for those displaced

title: Andy Burnham wins Makerfield by-election: What happens next with Greater Manchester mayoralty?

input.lead: Friday 19 June 2026 at 1:43pm Andy Burnham has been mayor of Greater Manchester for almost a decade.
input.summary: Friday 19 June 2026 at 1:43pm Andy Burnham has been mayor of Greater Manchester for almost a decade. Credit: PA Images Andy Burnham’s historic victory in the Makerfield by-election sets off a rapid chain reaction for both Greater Manchester and the wider UK political landscape A
input.evidence_text: Friday 19 June 2026 at 1:43pm Andy Burnham has been mayor of Greater Manchester for almost a decade. Credit: PA Images Andy Burnham’s historic victory in the Makerfield by-election sets off a rapid chain reaction for both Greater Manchester and the wider UK political landscape A metro mayor cannot simultaneously serve as an MP, a clear legal and electoral process is now underway to find someone new to take on the role Andy Burnham has won a seat as an MP - but can he still be the Mayor of Greater Manchester? No. Andy Burnham cannot be mayor and a member of parliament. He had seven days - from the moment of his election - to resign from the post which he has held for almost a decade He handed in his resignation on 19 June - triggering the process of the by-election Burnham cannot do both jobs because the Mayor of Greater Manchester holds Police and Crime Commissioner (PCC) powers and UK law strictly dictates that the office holder cannot simultaneously serve as an MP

input.evidence_text: Tuesday 21 April 2026 at 4:58pm Drivers at Greater Manchester Mental Health Trust will begin three further weeks of strikes. Credit: Jordan Pettitt/PA Mental health staff are set to strike over pay, UNISON has confirmed Drivers at Greater Manchester Mental Health Trust will begin three further weeks of strikes after bosses ignored their own report and failed to improve staff pay, the union reports The drivers - who transport samples, medication, linen and food across Greater Manchester – say they have already completed a job evaluation that found their roles should be upgraded to a higher pay grade But the union says bosses have not acted on the findings and have instead asked for a second opinion from another NHS trust. It says 11 drivers have been left thousands of pounds out of pocket UNISON North West regional organiser Paddy Cleary said: "Trust bosses have failed to act in time and workers have been left with no choice but to strike. Nobody wants to take this step, but staff are underpaid for the vital work they do – as the trust’s own report makes clear "The job evaluation process was followed correctly. These staff should be on a higher rate of pay. Instead of putting this right, senior managers have chosen to delay the inevitable by seeking unnecessary advice

input.evidence_text: A Wigan woman has been jailed for six years after a fatal dangerous driving incident on an icy morning Twenty six year old Megan Murphy from Lord Street in Ince got into her car at 5:47am on an icy winter morning and reversed from an alleyway onto Pembroke Road, Wigan, positioning her vehicle facing against the direction of the one-way street CCTV footage shows that Murphy’s windscreen and windows were visibly frozen, significantly obstructing her view of the road. As Murphy began to drive forward, she was unable to see Darryl Tomlinson lying in the road, who has collapsed hours earlier Two witnesses observed her driving towards Darryl. One witness ran towards Murphy’s vehicle, shouting “stop” and “go back,” while another sounded their car horn in an attempt to alert her Murphy brought her vehicle to a stop just inches from Darryl; however, due to the poor visibility caused by the frozen windows, she was unable to see what the witnesses were indicating She then drove forward, passing over Darryl, and moments later reversed back over him. She then proceeded forward causing the front wheel of her vehicle to travel over Darryl again. Darryl became trapped beneath the driver’s side of the car

title: Repurposed flats will help homeless Manchester families

input.lead: A block of flats in Wythenshawe is set to become the latest property to be repurposed to provide temporary accommodation for homeless families
input.summary: A block of flats in Wythenshawe is set to become the latest property to be repurposed to provide temporary accommodation for homeless families
input.evidence_text: A block of flats in Wythenshawe is set to become the latest property to be repurposed to provide temporary accommodation for homeless families The property in Greenwood Road, Woodhouse Park, has been acquired by the Council from registered housing provider Mosscare St Vincent’s as part of wider plans to boost the city’s stock of publicly-owned, quality temporary accommodation Work is due to start this summer, and be completed in December this year, to refurbish the property as 17 self-contained flats for homeless families from Wythenshawe and south Manchester All flats will be refurbished, including new kitchens and bathrooms in each case. Two new laundry rooms for shared use will also be created, and gardens to the rear will have raised bedding for residents’ use The new accommodation will see families supported by an accommodation team based on site to help them move on as quickly as possible into permanent settled tenancies The Greenwood Road initiative is the latest example of the Council’s drive to increase its temporary accommodation stock across the city to reduce the number of out-of-area placements, helping people who become homeless to stay closer to their previous homes and support networks

title: Innovative approach to speech and language support earns ministerial praise - Rochdale Borough Council

input.evidence_text: A government minister has praised Rochdale borough’s award-winning approach to speech, language and communication support which has seen waiting times for families significantly reduce This recognition came as part of a visit by the Minister of State for School Standards, Georgia Gould, who learned about the innovative new methods that have been introduced locally to help families, schools and early years settings to address children’s communication needs earli Children referred for speech and language therapy previously waited up to 44 weeks for a specialist appointment due to high levels of need in the borough However, local evidence found that in many cases children could often be supported sooner if families and professionals had access to practical tools and guidance at home or in settings like the classroom In response, Rochdale Borough Council and its NHS partners have introduced a new system of early intervention, which enables families and professionals to access instant speech and language support at the touch of a button This is thanks to things like artifical intelligence (AI) technology, tailored speech and language advice through WhatsApp, a coaching platform called Help at Hand, and an online virtual noticeboard which offers practical tips, resources and activities

title: Bolton Library marks centenary of the 1926 General Strike - Bolton Council

input.lead: Bolton Library and Museum is marking the centenary of one of the largest industrial disputes in British history with a special display
input.summary: Bolton Library and Museum is marking the centenary of one of the largest industrial disputes in British history with a special display
input.evidence_text: Bolton Library and Museum is marking the centenary of one of the largest industrial disputes in British history with a special display 100 years ago, in May 1926, millions of workers across Britain took part in the General Strike The strike originated as a dispute between coal mine owners and miners over reduced wages and longer working hours When the Trades Union Congress (TUC) declared their support for the miners, other industrial workers - including those in transport, engineering, and textiles - also walked out, and it became the first ever General Strike in British history The strike was called on 3 May 1926 and lasted nine days, where the first few days of the strike saw the transport network in Bolton grind to a halt without bus and train drivers Food deliveries were delayed, and some textile mills stopped operating

input.evidence_text: Wigan Council has submitted a planning application for a major refurbishment of Leigh Market, including proposals for a new roof, entrance, and lobby area Aware of the significant connection that the market has to the people of Leigh, the local authority included the building’s revamp as part of its Levelling Up (now Local Regeneration Fund) scheme As the planning application moves forward, preparatory works are also set to take place at several units within Spinning Gate Shopping Centre. Following discussions with traders, the market will be temporarily located to these units next year whilst the refurbishment of the main Councillor Dane Anderton, portfolio holder for economic development and regeneration, said: “The proposals for Leigh Market represent an exciting opportunity to refurbish a much-loved community venue that is home to a diverse group of traders “While these plans aim to protect the future of the market and put it in a much more stable condition, we want to ensure that the development works for both traders and shoppers alike. I’m pleased that after productive discussions with traders, the temporary market will be locate Boasting a long and proud market history, Leigh is believed to have held an outdoor market for hundreds of years. title: Marina Abramović: Balkan Erotic Epic | Aviva Studios

input.lead: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović .
input.summary: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović . Regarded as the world’s most influential performance a
input.evidence_text: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović . Regarded as the world’s most influential performance artist, Abramović’s transformative work has reached millions of people – from opening the Pyramid Stage at Glastonbury Festival t A four-hour ritual where ancient myth meets performance art, Balkan Erotic Epic explores the eroticism, spirituality and traditions of Abramović’s homeland through 13 visceral scenes Brought to life by a cast of over 70 performers, dancers, musicians and singers, the experience unfolds across Aviva Studios. Audiences are free to choose their own path – with pop-up encounters punctuating the action, bringing bodies together in dance, song and ritual Watch Fertility Rite , a fevered ritual where bodies writhe against the ground in a desperate call for fertility. Witness Massaging the Breast where women gesticulate over graves to awaken the earth. In Scaring the Gods , women bare themselves to the sky to banish storms. Listen Unafraid to push the boundaries of eroticism and performance art, Balkan Erotic Epic asks its audience a timeless question: What are our bodies truly for?

title: Tchaikovsky Symphony No 6

input.lead: Mel Bonis Ophélie Op 165 Emily Dunbar New work* Sergei Rachmaninov Rhapsody on a Theme of Paganini Op 43 Piotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky Symphony No 6 in B minor Op 74 ‘Pathétique’ Clemens Schuldt, Jasper Lecon* conductors Liana Storey piano RNCM Symphony Orchestra Tchaikovsky’s ‘ Pathé
input.summary: Mel Bonis Ophélie Op 165 Emily Dunbar New work* Sergei Rachmaninov Rhapsody on a Theme of Paganini Op 43 Piotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky Symphony No 6 in B minor Op 74 ‘Pathétique’ Clemens Schuldt, Jasper Lecon* conductors Liana Storey piano RNCM Symphony Orchestra Tchaikovsky’s ‘ Pathé
input.evidence_text: Mel Bonis Ophélie Op 165 Emily Dunbar New work* Sergei Rachmaninov Rhapsody on a Theme of Paganini Op 43 Piotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky Symphony No 6 in B minor Op 74 ‘Pathétique’ Clemens Schuldt, Jasper Lecon* conductors Liana Storey piano RNCM Symphony Orchestra Tchaikovsky’s ‘ Pathétique ’ Symphony stands as an unnerving prelude to the composer’s untimely death, which occurred just nine days after its premiere. Bleak yet epic, the true narrative behind this programmatic work is shrouded in as much mystery as the circumstances surroundin Tchaikovsky sets the tone with a haunting opening, before moving into a lilting yet unsettled waltz. The third movement creates the illusion of a triumphant conclusion, only for this hope to be undone in the final movement Perhaps a requiem to himself, the final Adagio lamentoso strays from the traditional symphonic form. Is this sombre, mournful elegy a resignation to fate?

input.evidence_text: The £35 million project set within 154 acres has transformed the former site of Worsley New Hall, restoring the historic walled garden, lake and surviving buildings Originally built for the 1st Earl of Ellesmere between 1840 and 1845, Worsley New Hall was a grand Gothic-style mansion, complete with glorious, formal landscaped gardens It’s now one of UK’s must-see gardens with so much to enjoy for visitors of all ages Step inside the magnificent 11-acre Weston Walled Garden, the site’s jewel in the crown. One of the largest Victorian walled gardens in the UK, it showcases a range of growing techniques and edible plants There’s also the spectacular Paradise Garden showcasing exotic planting inspired by Asiatic and Mediterranean gardens. The orchard and nearby bee and butterfly garden, have been designed to attract wildlife The Peel Learning Garden, supported by The Peel Group, will help schoolchildren learn how plants affect our lives and the planet, whilst the Community Wellbeing Garden provides a space for people to garden, socialise and relax

The move follows a significant decline in placements, with FE Wee To me, those two things are closely connected, for years, the conversation around skills and early careers has focused heavily on supply. More qualifications, more bootcamps, more people trained, more pathways into industry Those things matter. But creating talent is only half the equation. The other half is making sure employers have the capacity, confidence and support to absorb that talent Over the last year, Manchester Digital has been running TConnect, a pilot supported by the Gatsby Foundation and Greater Manchester Combined Authority that aimed to make it easier for employers to engage with T Level placements. Like most pilots, it raised as many questions as it One thing became clear very quickly. input.evidence_text: Fancy a space to connect with the community away from Canal Street? Kiki Family Social is a relaxed, grassroots meet-up with no rigid expectations, pressure, or hierarchy. The space is shaped by the people who come along — everyone helps make it what it is We meet every Thursday, and there’s always space at the table whether you’re new to Manchester or just dipping your toe into social meet-ups You’ll usually find a mix of familiar faces and new ones each week. We typically gather at a reserved table inside with pride flags, and there’s often a table outside too. On busy nights we’ll make sure flags are on the outside table as well. If you’re unsure where we are, just a 📅 Every Thursday, 7:30pm (You’re welcome to arrive earlier or later) 🍻 This isn’t a sober event, but there’s no pressure to drink. The bar offers zero-alcohol options as well as tea and coffee

input.evidence_text: Following the acclaimed production of An Ideal Husband , Broken Nose Theatre presents Marius von Mayenburg’s celebrated dark comedy The Ugly One , directed by Ramin Gray (Young Vic, RSC, Royal Opera). Twenty years after staging its premiere at the Royal Court, Gray returns to a p Lette appears to have it all: a successful career, a loving wife and a groundbreaking invention. But everything changes when he discovers a truth no one has ever dared to tell him — he is unbearably ugly. title: Андрей Кравченко

input.lead: Андрей Кравченко at Brixton Jamm
input.summary: Андрей Кравченко в Лондоне! 17 октября 2026 года в Brixton Jamm состоится концерт Андрея Кравченко в Лондоне
input.evidence_text: 17 октября 2026 года в Brixton Jamm состоится концерт Андрея Кравченко в Лондоне Андрей Кравченко покорил сердца тысяч слушателей благодаря своему уникальному голосу, харизме и способности превращать личные истории в песни, близкие каждому. В его творчестве смешиваются романтическая лирика, современное эстрадное звучание и проникновенная искренность После серии аншлаговых выступлений на самых престижных сценах Украины, среди которых Жовтневый Дворец и Дворец “Украина”, певец продолжает свое концертное путешествие и приглашает поклонников на особую встречу в Лондоне Концерты Андрея Кравченко известны своей теплой атмосферой: здесь нет дистанции между сценой и залом, ведь каждое выступление превращается в откровенный разговор на языке музыки. Гостей ждут любимые хиты, живой звук, море эмоций и моменты, которые захочется сохранить в памяти над Важной частью творческой миссии артиста остается поддержка Украины — часть средств от концерта будет передана на помощь ВСУ! Подарите себе вечер настоящей романтики, красивой музыки и искренних чувств вместе с Андреем Кравченко

title: Monatik Europe Tour 2027. London

input.lead: Monatik Europe Tour 2027. London at Indigo at The O2
input.summary: Live-концерт MONATIK в Лондоне! 26 февраля 2027 года Indigo at The O2 превратится в место музыки, ритма и свободы, ведь на сцену выйдет вечно танцующий, невероятный исполнитель MONATIK!
input.evidence_text: 26 февраля 2027 года Indigo at The O2 превратится в место музыки, ритма и свободы, ведь на сцену выйдет вечно танцующий, невероятный исполнитель MONATIK! Этот концерт — больше, чем просто выступление: это пространство, где публика сливается в едином ритме, проживая что-то настоящ MONATIK отправляется в международный концертный тур, в рамках которого посетит Великобританию! Артист влюбил миллионы слушателей в свою музыку благодаря песням “Кожний раз”, “А що?”, “Сильно”, “Кружить” и многим другим, в каждой из которых ритм занимает центральное место. title: Innovation Office Hours with Naomi Timperley - for founders, startups, scale-ups, innovation leaders and ambitious businesses looking to grow
source_url:
https://events.compiledmcr.com/

Overview

Join Naomi for a 30-minute one-to-one for founders, startups, scale-ups, innovation leaders and ambitious businesses looking to grow.

Expert Office Hours - for founders, startups, scale-ups, innovation leaders and ambitious businesses looking to grow

Sign up for a 30-minute one-to-one at The Tech Hub at No.1 Circle Square hosted by Bruntwood SciTech.

Expert Office Hours with Naomi Timperley

Building a business can be exciting, but it can also be lonely. Sometimes what you need isn't another webinar or workshop, you need someone independent to help you sense-check your thinking, challenge assumptions, unlock opportunities and identify the next steps.

Join Naomi Timperley for a free 30-minute one-to-one session.

Naomi has spent more than 15 years supporting founders, startups, scale-ups and innovation-led organisations. She is Co-Founder of Tech North Advocates, UK Lead for Global Tech Advocates, Co-Founder of End Game and was named Computer Weekly's Most Influential Woman in UK Tech 2025.

Whether you're looking to:

• Validate a new idea or business model
• Navigate growth challenges
• Explore AI adoption and innovation opportunities
• Build strategic partnerships
• Improve your proposition and positioning
• Access funding, investment or support programmes
• Expand your network and connections
• Sense-check a key business decision
• Work out what comes next

These informal office hours are an opportunity to discuss your challenges, opportunities and ambitions and leave with practical advice, useful connections and clear next steps.

Suitable for founders, startups, scale-ups, innovation leaders and ambitious businesses looking to grow.

Use the booking form to book a slot. Time slots will be for 30 minutes.

See you there!

title: Scaling Innovation Across Borders
source_url:
https://events.compiledmcr.com/

Manchester’s tech and investment scene is firing on all cylinders, but what happens when we look beyond our own backyard to drive the next wave of growth

input.lead: Scaling Innovation Across Borders

enrichment_health.status:
failed: URL can't contain control characters. "/

Manchester’s tech and investment scene is firing on all cylinders, but what happens when we look beyond our own backyard to drive the next wave of growth?


This Thursday Gathering with Sister explores what happens when you take the exciting momentum in Greater Manchester and link up with international partners. With a spotlight on connecting Ireland and Manchester, we’ll be discussing how to turn the growing ties between these two thriving ecosystems into the next big opportunity for local businesses.\xa0


Whether you're a founder just starting to build, an investor looking for the next breakout, or a business leader who hasn’t thought about international growth yet, this session is for you.


You’ll hear from experts from both sides of the Irish sea, including Invest Manchester, Innovation Exchange Ireland, Uniquely, Guinness Enterprise Centre, ClimbUK and Platform94 on how regional and cross-border partnerships and shared talent can fast-track your progress.\xa0

title: Bioengineering Pathways: Academia and Entrepreneurship
source_url:
https://events.compiledmcr.com/

The session will highlight how scientists can navigate careers spanning academia, industry, and entrepreneurship. Drawing on the experiences of our speakers, we will explore complementary career trajectories, including:

  • ​Transitioning across international biotech ecosystems and startup environments

  • ​Combining academic leadership with company co-founding

  • ​Bridging fundamental research with commercial application, particularly in sustainability and climate biotech

​Whether you are exploring commercialisation, developing new technologies, or considering future career directions, join us to hear inspiring stories and learn how Nucleate UK partners with Advanced Research and Invention Agency (ARIA) to accelerate ambitious ideas.

Speakers:
Lucia Burdisso, PhD - Lucia is the Chief Scientific Officer at Deep Blue Biotech, where she leads the development of a cyanobacterial synthetic biology platform for sustainable, CO₂-based chemical production. A biotechnologist with a PhD and more than 10 years of experience in R&D across academia and biotech startups, Lucia has worked in 10 laboratories across 4 countries, including startups in Argentina and the San Francisco Bay Area, and is now based in Sheffield.

Ressa Lebedinec, PhD - Ressa is a molecular mycologist and Senior Biomolecular Engineer at The University of Manchester Biotechnology Institute, with over 10 years of experience in fungal biology, biomolecular engineering, and sustainable biotechnology. She is the co-founder of mushroom+, a deep-tech platform formed in 2025 within the Carbon13 venture builder ecosystem.
mushroom+ is developing technologies that enhance the protein content and performance of mushrooms, transforming existing mushroom farms into climate-positive protein hubs. By increasing protein yield and nutritional density per kilogram of biomass, the company aims to address critical environmental and economic challenges in food production, without requiring the food system to be rebuilt from scratch.

Daniele Parisi, PhD - Daniele is a Senior Scientist at C3, where he leads efforts in scaling up fermentation processes and solving challenges related to circular biomanufacturing. With over a decade of experience in sustainability-driven research and development, he brings deep expertise in fermentation, downstream processing (DSP), and biocatalysis. Daniele holds a PhD in Molecular Biology and Biochemistry, and has contributed to both early-stage sustainability-focused startups and global companies, including his recent work with Croda.

Drinks and mexican food will be provided during the networking session with peers and potential collaborators.

title: CONEXEN Manchester July
source_url:
https://events.compiledmcr.com/

Registration needed: https:/conexen.net/conexen-events/conexen-manchester-july/

Welcome to CONEXEN Manchester – A warm and welcoming community for professionals and founders in biopharma, life sciences, and health tech in Manchester! Here, we invite you to join us for an informal and friendly gathering where connections are made, knowledge is shared, and friendships are formed. Come with an open heart and a willingness to “listen” rather than “sell.”

​​We will be hosting our Manchester event at the amazing space of Cloud 23 Cocktail Bar. It is a casual setting where we can come together, exchange greetings, engage in conversation, and simply enjoy a pleasant time.

​​Picture this as more than just an event, it’s a life sciences community gathering. Our focus is on providing you with an opportunity to meet fellow professionals and founders in a relaxed and amiable environment. We’ll share conversations over delicious food and drinks, and leave with a sense of excitement and a pocketful of new contacts and ideas.

​​Join us for an evening surrounded by incredible people, and let’s make it a night to remember. Looking forward to sharing good company and creating memorable connections!

​Theme: The Forces Reshaping Work & Culture in Life Sciences.

Join a focused discussion led by Paul Bradley-Law on the forces redefining culture in a hybrid world. Our panellists will explore how hybrid working, evolving HR trends, leadership style, and a multi-generational workforce are reshaping how organisations operate, alongside the growing influence of AI in the workplace.

We’ll examine how trust is built through clarity and candour in uncertain times—without defaulting to micromanagement—and how to address “proximity bias” to ensure fair visibility, recognition, and opportunity across distributed teams.

Join peers shaping the North-West Life Sciences ecosystem and driving the region’s growing influence.

​Moderator: Paul Bradley-Law (Founder, The Great People Machine)

​Panelists:

  • Ike Cooke, Business Growth & Commercialisation Manager, LYVA Labs

  • Julia Buckler, Senior Director, Site Head, Qiagen

​The format of the night:

  • ​18:00 Doors open – drinks, networking and meeting great people.

  • ​18:30 Talks start

  • ​19:15 Drinks, food and networking.

  • ​20:30 End of the night – you leave with new contacts and fresh ideas.

​​Venue: Cloud 23 Cocktail Bar – The Manchester Deansgate Hotel, 303 Deansgate, Manchester M3 4LQ, United Kingdom.

This event is proudly supported by Nexa Life Sciences and Kadans Science Partner, hosted by Kadans Science Partner.

​

​And finally…

​This is a ticketed event. Food and drinks for the night are included.

​Please let your colleagues and industry friends know about us, we hope to build it into a great resource for the ecosystem over the coming months and years. Attendance will be taken at this event. If you register but don’t show up, this has a knock-on effect on event arrangements. If it happens 3 times, we may need to reconsider your participation. This is simply a measure to allow appropriate respect to the organisers, speakers, sponsors, and your fellow Conexers.

​See you there!

​Ashley, Julia, and Sajid
Manchester Organisers

​Tickets FAQs: https:/www.conexen.net/faq

Questions

title: Unlocking New Possibilities with the Oracle AI Ecosystem
source_url:
https://events.compiledmcr.com/

An executive innovation experience, connecting AI, data and applications to real business outcomes.

Unlocking New Possibilities with the Oracle AI Ecosystem

Join us at Oracle's offices in Moorgate, London for Kovaion Connect 2026 - a focused executive briefing showing how Oracle Applications, Data, AI, OCI and Oracle APEX come together to help organisations innovate faster, operate smarter and unlock measurable business outcomes.

This is a half-day session built for senior decision-makers, combining thought leadership, the latest Oracle AI product updates, live demonstrations and one-to-one advisory conversations. The morning briefing moves from strategy to live innovation, followed by a networking lunch and optional private advisory slots in the afternoon. One Oracle. More value. Limitless innovation.

What you can expect

  • Executive insights into how AI, data and enterprise applications are reshaping the way organisations operate

  • The newest embedded AI capabilities across Oracle Fusion Applications

  • Release highlights and AI enhancements in Oracle APEX that accelerate enterprise innovation

  • Live demos of intelligent enterprise applications built on Oracle APEX and Oracle AI

  • A practical view of building an AI-ready data foundation with unified analytics

  • Private 1:1 executive advisory discussions with Oracle and Kovaion experts (afternoon, by request)

  • A networking lunch to exchange perspectives with peers and specialists

The day at a glance

  • 09:30 – 13:30 - Main session and networking lunch

  • 13:30 – 17:00 - Optional 1:1 executive advisory sessions

Who should attend

This briefing is designed for CIOs and CTOs, Heads of Applications, IT and Operations, Data and AI leaders, and Transformation heads - the people setting the enterprise technology, AI and digital strategy agenda.

Featured speakers include

  • Simon Hunt - Head of Product Management, Oracle APEX, Oracle UK

  • Mohana Priya Venkatachalam - Chief Projects Officer, Kovaion UK

  • Srinivas Nadella - Strategic Accounts Manager, Kovaion UK

With more Oracle and industry speakers to be announced.

You can view full agenda here - https:/www.kovaion.com/kovaion-connect-2026/

Venue: Oracle Corporation UK Ltd., One South Place, London EC2M 2RB - minutes from Moorgate, Liverpool Street and Bank stations, with rail, Underground and Elizabeth line connections.

enrichment_health.status:

Abstract;
In this age of AI, where AI permeates our every system. Do we need to think differently about AI. Do we need to move beyond what it CAN do to what it SHOULD do and HOW and for WHOM?

Speaker:
Stuart Coulson is the Principal Cyber Security Service Manager at a global telecoms giant, the founder of HiddenText, and someone who’s spent 20+ years turning complexity into clarity. He doesn’t do fluff. Whether mentoring start-ups or advising boardrooms, he challenges lazy thinking, strips away jargon, and leaves people with ideas they can actually use. Recognised as Cyber Mentor of the Year (2023) and a Top 50 Tech Leader (2025), Stuart’s mission is simple: build resilience, grow leaders, and make mentoring a culture that outlives him.

title: Female Founders North Summer Social - Manchester
source_url:
https://events.compiledmcr.com/

Join us on the terrace at Neo for a relaxed social evening with drinks and nibbles in our beautiful mezzanine lounge.

Female Founders North brings together digital and tech founders from across the region in a safe, supportive environment to share stories, experiences, and build meaningful connections through informal networking.

This is your opportunity to:

  • Expand your tech and digital network

  • Discuss barriers, challenges, and opportunities with fellow entrepreneurs

  • Connect with founders who have navigated similar journeys or are currently on their entrepreneurial path

  • Be part of a thriving community that's been running successfully in Leeds for over two years and recently expanded to Manchester

Bruntwood SciTech has successfully delivered Female Founders Incubators across Leeds, Manchester, and Alderley Park, helping startups grow and supporting the next generation of women-owned tech businesses.

This event is designed for:

input.evidence_text: Manchester inward investment: ‘Reasons for optimism’ despite fall in FDI schemes City attracted 31 FDI projects in 2025 – down from 44 in 2024 Economic Development Alistair Houghton Editor, Business Live 05:00, 23 Jun 2026 Manchester City Centre se Manchester was Britain's best-performing city outside London for attracting Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) in 2025 despite a fall in the number of projects, new figures from EY have shown The latest EY 2026 UK Attractiveness Survey, which ranked 259 European regions on the number of FDI projects they attracted last year, showed Manchester attracted 31 FDI projects in 2025 – down from 44 in 2024. That made it the best-performing city outside of London for FDI for t Key FDI sectors included software and IT services, with six projects, with four projects in the health and social work sector and another four in finance, business and professional services The UK saw a total of 730 FDI projects last year, down 14% on 853 in 2024. The North West was the fourth ranked region in the UK in terms of project numbers with 51, behind Greater London (279 projects), Scotland (108 projects) and the West Midlands (68 projects) Of the UK’s nations and regions, only Greater London, Wales and Northern Ireland saw year-on-year growth in FDI projects, with the South West flat. All other regions reported declines, with the North West seeing a 41% year-on-year fall

input.evidence_text: Economic Development Andy Burnham wins Makerfield: When will the next Greater Manchester mayor be elected now he is an MP? Huge win in by-election paves way for Labour leadership challenge Economic Development Declan Carey Local Democracy Reporter 07:33, 19 Jun 2026 Andy Burnham Andy Burnham is heading back to Parliament after winning the Makerfield by-election The newly elected Labour MP came out on top in the race against a strong challenge from Reform UK candidate Robert Kenyon But overnight Mr Burnham secured a seismic win, with 24,927 votes placing him well ahead of Reform challenger Robert Kenyon who secured 15,696 votes. His return to Parliament is expected to pave the way for a leadership challenge against Prime Minister Keir Starmer It marks the end of weeks of campaigning in the constituency based south of Wigan, and the start of the next political race in Greater Manchester for Andy Burnham's old job as mayor Becoming an MP disqualifies Burnham from being Greater Manchester mayor, so a replacement needs to be found for the region's top political job
